channel.title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
channel.description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

item.title: Jul 18: Ukraine Enters the Ledger as the Acting Party in a Civilian-Harm Event for the First Time
item.description: Fifteen countries held their published scores steady tonight, the seventh straight cycle without a single score change. Tonight's most important finding involves Ukraine, not Russia. Ukrainian drones struck two Russian logistics warehouses on July 18, killing nine people. Seven of the dead were civi…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 17: A Fact-Check Finds Afghanistan's Zero Score Has No Fresh Evidence Behind It Tonight
item.description: Fifteen countries and one artificial-intelligence company held their published scores steady tonight, the sixth straight cycle without a single score change. Tonight's most important finding is a fact-check, not a score move. A war-crimes warrant against Taliban leaders, which earlier reporting link…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 16: Sudan Holds at Zero as the European Parliament Votes to Label the RSF a Terrorist Organization
item.description: Fifteen countries and one artificial-intelligence company held their scores steady tonight, the fifth straight cycle without a single score change. Cuba's independent prisoner-monitoring group confirmed a record 1,306 political prisoners, but the benchmark treats the rise as a continuation of an alr…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 15: Eritrea's First Individual Review Confirms Its Zero Score Reflects Real Conduct
item.description: Eritrea received its first individual review tonight, after sitting at zero out of 100 for months as an unreviewed placeholder score. The review lands on the same zero, because the United Nations says Eritrea's government is still committing crimes against humanity, including indefinite forced milit…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 14: A UN Mission Formally Finds Sudan's El Fasher Killings Bear 'Distinct Markers of Genocide'
item.description: A UN Fact-Finding Mission formally found that Rapid Support Forces atrocities in El Fasher, Sudan bear 'distinct markers of genocide' and opened an urgent new inquiry into a similar siege forming around El Obeid. Sudan's score was already at zero out of 100, the lowest possible mark, so this grave f…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 13: Tunisia's Proposed Score Cut Is Reaffirmed for a Fourth Day With No New Escalation
item.description: Tunisia's proposed score cut, from 34.4 to 23.8 out of 100, was reaffirmed for a fourth straight day. No new escalation emerged beyond the mass sentencing of 21 opposition figures already behind the proposal, so the published score stays at 34.4 while the benchmark's editors review the finding. Iran…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 12: Anthropic Holds at 59.1 as a Chinese Regulator's Backdoor Claim Fails the Benchmark's Own Test
item.description: Anthropic's published score holds at 59.1 out of 100 after a Chinese government cybersecurity platform alleged that its Claude Code tool contains an undisclosed 'backdoor.' The benchmark checked the claim against its own rule for when such a finding can move a score: it needs either a genuine second…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 11: Tunisia's Pending Score Review Is Reaffirmed for a Second Day
item.description: Tunisia's pending score review was reaffirmed for a second day. New evidence from the United Nations Human Rights Council's closing session on July 8, plus further detail on the 25-year prison sentence and roughly $600 million fine against former Truth and Dignity Commission president Sihem Bensedri…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 10: Tunisia's First Individual Review Proposes a Score of 23.8, Down From a Never-Checked 34.4
item.description: Tunisia received its first individual assessment on July 10. Its published score of 34.4 out of 100 turned out to be a placeholder that no analyst had actually checked. The new evidence-based score is 23.8 out of 100, a drop of 10.6 points, but Tunisia stays in the same performance band, so this is…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 9: A UN Inquiry Finds Sudan's Conduct Meets the Legal Test for Genocide; Myanmar's War Passes 100,000 Deaths
item.description: A United Nations inquiry concluded on July 9 that mass killings and gang rapes committed by Sudan's Rapid Support Forces (RSF) meet the legal definition of genocide. Sudan already scores 0 of 100, the lowest possible mark, so the finding cannot push the score down further -- but it is the most serio…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 8: Turkey and Kenya Both Enter Tonight's List After Crackdowns Tied to High-Visibility Events
item.description: July 8, 2026 is the third consecutive confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores, and the pending-change queue stays empty for a third straight day. Turkey enters tonight's list at 10.3 of 100 on a mass crackdown of 209-225 arrests around the Ankara NATO sum…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 7: Venezuela's Earthquake Toll Hits 3,535 on Day 13 — a Response Called "the Last Responder" Stays Just Under the Threshold for a Score Change; Cuba's Third Blackout in Six Months Adds a New Watch
item.description: July 7, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed countries hold at their published scores. Zero new score-change proposals were filed, and the queue of pending changes stays empty for a second straight day. Venezuela's earthquake death toll reached 3,535 people on day 13, up from 2,645…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 6: Russia 0 of 100: July Civilian Toll Hits 170 Per Day — Highest Since 2023; Kyiv Jul 5-6 Kills 21-Plus, Zero of 23 Missiles Intercepted; Dnipro Strike Kills 17; EU Special Tribunal Active; Ukraine 50.0 of 100 Confirmed; South Sudan 0 of 100 — Famine Risk in Four Counties
item.description: July 6, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The pending queue entered July 6 empty — El Salvador's downgrade to 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band was applied on July 5, 2026, the first score change since th…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 5: Sudan 0 of 100: UN Red Alert Active Day 2 as RSF El Obeid Siege Tightens — 500,000 at Imminent Risk; El Fasher Crimes Against Humanity on Record; Floor Reinforced
item.description: July 5, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward as pending for a fourt…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 4: Iran 2.5 of 100: Khamenei Funeral Opens July 4 — First Supreme Leader Transition in 35 Years; Mojtaba as Leading Successor; US-Iran Talks Paused to July 11; Protest Watch Active
item.description: July 4, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forward as pending for a third…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 3: Russia 0 of 100: Record July Civilian Toll — 286 Killed, 1,388 Injured; EU Special Tribunal Active; Floor Reinforced
item.description: July 3, 2026 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: all 15 assessed entities hold at their published scores. Zero new proposals were generated. The El Salvador downgrade proposal — first formal assessment proposing 15.0 of 100 in the Critical band against a published score of 20.3 of 100 — carries forwar…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 2: El Salvador 20.3 of 100 Published — First Formal Assessment Proposes 15.0 of 100 Critical Band; Developing-to-Critical Band Crossing Proposed; Published Score Unchanged Pending Review
item.description: El Salvador received its first formal benchmark assessment on July 2, 2026. The assessment generates a proposed score of 15.0 of 100 — the Critical band — against a seed score of 20.3 of 100 that was never formally evaluated. The proposed delta is -5.3, a Developing-to-Critical band crossing. The ev…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jul 1: China 19.5 of 100: Ethnic Unity and Progress Law in Force July 1 — Article 63 Diaspora Liability Active; Hong Kong's Last Pro-Democracy Party Dissolved
item.description: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The law codifies existing assimilation policy, activates Article 63 extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members, and coincides with the dissolution of Hong Kong's last pro-democracy party. Both Amnesty International and Human R…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 30: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Spread to a Fourth Province June 30 — 1,307 Cases, 377 Deaths; Haut-Uele Now Borders South Sudan at the Absolute Floor
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak spread to Haut-Uele province on June 30 — a fourth province affected — raising confirmed cases to 1,307 and deaths to 377. Haut-Uele borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic. South Sudan sits at the absolute floor at 0 o…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 29: Kuwait 25.0 and Bahrain 9.4 of 100: Iran Struck Both June 28 — Scores Unchanged; Harm Is Iran's Conduct, Not the Targets'
item.description: Iran fired missiles and drones at the US military base in Kuwait and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain on June 28. Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles — no deaths, two minor shrapnel injuries. A drone strike in Muharraq, Bahrain damaged a home — no casualties. Neither government's sco…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 28: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Twin Earthquakes Killed 1,430-Plus — Government Accepted Aid, But Relief Was Slow and Opaque
item.description: June 28 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 12 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record. The lead story is Venezuela. Twin magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 earthquakes struck on June 24, killing more than 1,430 people and leaving about 68,900 families report…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 27: Lebanon 17.2 of 100: Framework Signed June 26, Rejected by Hezbollah Within Hours — War Continues
item.description: June 27 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and 11 false-alarm signals stopped — a new single-night record, eclipsing the eight from the night before. The lead story is a diplomatic agreement that did not hold. Secretary Rubio announced an Israel-Lebanon framework o…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 26: Israel 0 of 100: UN Inquiry Finds Genocide Against Palestinian Children — About 20,000 Killed
item.description: June 26 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 15 assessments, zero score changes, and eight false-alarm signals stopped — doubling the previous single-night record set June 25. The cycle's lead finding is a landmark UN document. On June 23, the UN Commission of Inquiry (report A/HRC/62/CRP.2) found that…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 25: AI and Health Companies Face a Wave of Legal Actions — Scores Hold Until Courts Decide
item.description: June 25 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and four false-alarm signals stopped before they could distort the record. The cycle's defining theme is a wave of legal and regulatory actions against AI and health companies — none of which has yet produced a concluded p…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 24: Yemen 0 of 100: Houthi Authorities Detained 69 or More UN Staff — One Died in Custody
item.description: June 24 is a confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments, zero score changes, and three false-alarm signals caught and discarded before they could distort the record. The most significant new evidence is from Yemen, where Houthi authorities arbitrarily detained at least 69 United Nations staff membe…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 23: Microsoft 65.3 Positive Watch: Covington Investigation Confirms MoD Azure Surveillance — Subscription Terminated, Five Governance Measures Enacted
item.description: June 23 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 19 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 19 confirmations, and 8 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining analytical feature is the inversion of the usual floor-cluster narrative: the highest-salience finding is a positive gove…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 22: South Sudan 0.0 Reinforced: 2018 Peace Deal Collapses toward Total War — UN Commission Documents Aerial Bombardment, 471,000 New IDPs
item.description: June 22 is a zero-applied, confirmation-dominant cycle: 20 assessments across 3 indexes, 0 score changes, 20 confirmations, and 9 floor or near-floor reinforcements. The defining feature is the simultaneous depth of adverse evidence across four independent geographic crises: Sudan, South Sudan, DRC,…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 21: Humana Band Crossing Applied: 40.6 Functional → 35.2 Developing on DOJ Disability-Discrimination Complaint and AI Denial Class Action
item.description: June 21 is a single-downgrade cycle across 20 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 1 applied band crossing (Humana, Functional → Developing) and 19 confirmations with 7 floor/near-floor reinforcements. Humana's score moves to 35.2 Developing (from 40.6 Functional, -5.4, rank 180 → 305), applied today…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 20: University Index First Nightly Cycle: 10 Universities Assessed — All Hold via Victim/Perpetrator Attribution
item.description: June 20 is a zero-proposal cycle across 21 assessments in 3 indexes, producing 21 confirmations and 6 floor reinforcements. The analytically dominant event is the University Index's first nightly scan: 10 universities assessed as priority refinements, all holding at published composites. The methodo…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 19: Nigeria 21.9 → 18.0 (Δ -3.9): IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe for ~15,000 Borno Residents — Band Crossing Developing → Critical on Sub-Threshold Delta
item.description: Two formal score change proposals tonight: Figure AI moves from 37.5 to 31.3 (Δ -6.2) in the AI labs index, remaining in the Developing band; and Nigeria moves from 21.9 to 18.0 (Δ -3.9) in the countries index, crossing the Developing-to-Critical band boundary under the band-crossing rule — which ap…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark
